rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth ask (verifying device_communicator/reduce_scatter for _TP too, not just PCP/DP/EP), so recall is low for both. X is tighter and more precise — its single inline comment and API-level claims (DPMetadata fields, use_device_communicator default) are well-grounded, but it offers little concrete actionable change beyond a pass/fail table. Y digs deeper with grep/read_file evidence and flags a real coverage gap (no test exercises the _num_tokens_pre_hook wiring) plus the same stale dp_metadata caching risk X noticed, giving it more file/line-anchored actionable items, though two of its five findings (docstring vs. collective-comment) are near-duplicates and the 'major' severity on a coverage nit is inflated. Net: Y's broader, more actionable coverage edges out X's cleaner but thinner review.
